title: Government to Replace WPI with Producer Price Index Over Five Years
content:
🏛️ India's Inflation Measurement Reform — WPI to PPI Transition

👤 Who → Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

📋 What → India begins a five-year transition from Wholesale Price Index (WPI) to Producer Price Index (PPI)

📍 Where → India (national policy)

🎯 Purpose → To modernise India's inflation measurement framework with a more comprehensive producer-level price index

📌 Important Facts
▪️ Revised WPI series with base year 2022-23 to be released on 15 June 2026 (replacing 2011-12 base)
▪️ Revised WPI basket expands from 697 to 957 commodities (adding solar energy, wind energy, nuclear electricity)
▪️ New indices introduced: Output PPI, Trial Input PPI, Service PPI (7 sectors: banking, insurance, pension fund management, securities transactions, railways, air passenger transport, telecommunications)
▪️ WPI and PPI published simultaneously for 5 years; after that WPI discontinued, PPI becomes primary
▪️ PPI captures prices at producer level (farm-gate/factory-gate); includes goods AND services
▪️ Output PPI covers 123 product categories (~1,500 items initially)

⚠️ Exam Angle → Base year change (2011-12 to 2022-23), commodity count (697 to 957), revised WPI launch date (15 June 2026), and WPI vs PPI distinction are high-probability MCQ topics

It is the State Animal of Tamil Nadu, endemic to the Western Ghats, with largest habitats in Grass Hills NP (Anamalai TR) and Mukurthi NP. #07-08June2026

question: RBI imposed a penalty on Canara Bank for failing to upload KYC records to CKYCR. What does CKYCR stand for?
options: A) Central Know Your Customer Registry
explanation: CKYCR stands for Central KYC Records Registry — a centralised repository of KYC records maintained by CERSAI (Central Registry of Securitisation Asset Reconstruction and Security Interest of India). RBI imposed a penalty of ₹41.80 lakh on Canara Bank for violations related to KYC norms and Unclaimed Deposits/Inoperative Accounts. #07-08June2026

question: PFRDA launched StAR NPS, a new digital onboarding platform. Which organisation developed this platform?
explanation: StAR NPS (Securities Trading and Reporting – National Pension System) was developed by BSE Technologies Private Limited. It is a digital onboarding platform operationalized by PFRDA to make NPS enrollment faster, paperless, and more convenient. It will be used by Points of Presence (PoPs) and their pension agents. #07-08June2026

question: IFC committed $50 million to Hygenco Green Energies for green hydrogen development in India. IFC is the private-sector arm of which organisation?
explanation: The International Finance Corporation (IFC) is the private-sector investment arm of the World Bank Group. IFC committed $50 million to Hygenco Green Energies. Together with other investors (Siemens Financial Services, Clean Technology Fund, Frontier Opportunities Fund, Fullerton Carbon Action Fund), the total investment in Hygenco amounts to $105 million. #07-08June2026

question: India's BHASHINI Division signed an MoU with which institution of Nepal to develop an AI-powered multilingual language platform?
options: D) Nepal Academy of Science and Technology
explanation: Digital India BHASHINI Division (DIBD) under MeitY signed an MoU with Kathmandu University's Centre for Digital Public Infrastructure and Artificial Intelligence (DPI-AI) to develop multilingual language technologies and Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for improved digital inclusion in India and Nepal. #07-08June2026

question: Lionel Messi became the first individual footballer to win the Princess of Asturias Award for Sports. In which country is this award presented annually?
explanation: The Princess of Asturias Awards are annual awards presented in Spain to individuals and institutions making exceptional contributions in various fields. Lionel Messi became the first individual footballer to receive this award for Sports. Notable past recipients include Rafael Nadal, Michael Schumacher, Serena Williams, and Eliud Kipchoge. #07-08June2026

question: R. Vaishali won the WR Chess Women's Rapid Tournament held in Tokyo. She became India's third woman Grandmaster in which year?
explanation: R. Vaishali became India's third woman Grandmaster in 2023. She won the WR Chess Women's Rapid Tournament in Tokyo by defeating Alua Nurman of Kazakhstan in the final. #07-08June2026

question: Alexander Zverev won the French Open 2026 men's singles title. He became the first German man to win a Grand Slam singles title since Boris Becker. In which year did Boris Becker win his last Grand Slam?
explanation: Alexander Zverev (Germany) won his maiden Grand Slam title at French Open 2026 by defeating Italy's Flavio Cobolli. He became the first German man since Boris Becker (Australian Open 1996) to win a Grand Slam singles title — a gap of 30 years. #07-08June2026

question: Kimi Antonelli became the youngest driver to win the Monaco Grand Prix 2026 at the age of 19 years, 9 months, and 13 days. Whose previous record did he break?
explanation: Kimi Antonelli (Mercedes) won the Monaco Grand Prix 2026 at 19 years, 9 months, 13 days — breaking Lewis Hamilton's record of 23 years, 4 months, 18 days. Antonelli has won 5 consecutive races in the 2026 F1 season: China, Japan, Miami, Canada, and Monaco. #07-08June2026

question: Malayalam actor Salim Kumar, who recently passed away, won the National Film Award for Best Actor for his performance in which film?
explanation: National Award-winning Malayalam actor Salim Kumar won the National Film Award for Best Actor for his performance in Adaminte Makan Abu. He passed away recently. Veteran film producer and former CBFC Chairperson Pahlaj Nihalani also passed away around the same time. #07-08June2026

question: Vice-President C. P. Radhakrishnan released the book 'When Audit Matters: CAG Interventions That Made a Difference'. Who edited this book?
explanation: The book 'When Audit Matters: CAG Interventions That Made a Difference' was edited by Vinod Rai, former Comptroller and Auditor General of India. It was released by Vice-President C. P. Radhakrishnan. #07-08June2026

question: World Oceans Day is observed on 8 June every year. What percentage of the world's oxygen do oceans produce?
explanation: World Oceans Day is observed on 8 June with the 2026 theme 'Reimagine: Beyond the world we know, a new relationship with our ocean'. Oceans cover more than 70% of Earth's surface and produce at least 50% of the world's oxygen. It supports SDG 14 – Life Below Water. #07-08June2026

options: A) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2004-05
B) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2011-12
C) It is the first IIP data released under new base year 2022-23
D) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2017-18
answer: C) It is the first IIP data released under new base year 2022-23
explanation: April 2026 IIP (4.9% growth) is the first data released under the new base year 2022-23, replacing the old 2011-12 series. Released by MoSPI. The new series expanded the item basket to 463 items. Base years 2004-05, 2011-12, and 2017-18 are distractors referencing older or non-existent revisions. #02June2026

title: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged at 5.25%
content:

📋 What → MPC held its 61st meeting and voted unanimously to keep the Policy Repo Rate unchanged at 5.25%

🎯 Purpose → To maintain monetary stability while supporting economic growth

▪️ Policy Repo Rate: 5.25% (unchanged)

▪️ Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Rate: 5.50%

▪️ Real GDP growth projected for 2026-27: 6.6%
▪️ CPI Inflation projected for 2026-27: 5.1%
▪️ MPC stance: Neutral (continued)
▪️ This was the 61st MPC meeting

title: Surha Tal Becomes India's 100th Ramsar Site
content:
🏛️ UP Wetland Designated as India's 100th Ramsar Site on World Environment Day

👤 Who → Jai Prakash Narayan Bird Sanctuary (Surha Tal), Ballia district, Uttar Pradesh

📋 What → Designated as India's 100th Ramsar Site (Wetland of International Importance)

📍 Where → Ballia district, Uttar Pradesh

🎯 Purpose → Recognition of ecological importance of the wetland under the Ramsar Convention

📌 Important Facts
▪️ India's 100th Ramsar Site
▪️ 13th Ramsar Site of Uttar Pradesh
▪️ 2,595th Ramsar Site in the world
▪️ Tamil Nadu leads with 20 Ramsar Sites
▪️ Gujarat has the largest wetland area in India
▪️ India has the highest number of Ramsar Sites in Asia and 3rd highest globally (after UK – 176 and Mexico – 144)
▪️ Ramsar Convention signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971
▪️ Designated on World Environment Day

⚠️ Exam Angle → India's 100th Ramsar site name (Surha Tal/JP Bird Sanctuary), its district (Ballia, UP), global rank (2,595th), Tamil Nadu's count (20), and Ramsar Convention year (1971) are prime MCQ targets

question: UPI hit a record high in May 2026 with 23.2 billion transactions. NPCI, which oversees UPI, is a joint initiative of which two bodies?
options: A) SEBI and Ministry of Finance
B) RBI and Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
D) Ministry of Finance and DPIIT
answer: B) RBI and Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
explanation: NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) is a joint initiative of RBI and the Indian Banks' Association (IBA). It is an umbrella body for retail payment & settlement systems. UPI processed 23.2 billion transactions worth ₹29.90 lakh crore in May 2026 — a record high. SEBI, NITI Aayog, DPIIT have no role in NPCI's establishment. #02June2026
